title: Scams and Phishing
drDigitalIntro: Phishing is when criminals send fake emails or messages pretending to be a trusted company — your bank, a big company, or the government. They want you to click a link and enter your password or personal information. Warning signs: urgent language ('Your account will be closed!'), unexpected requests for passwords, sender addresses that are slightly off (bankk.com instead of bank.com), and links that don't match the company's real website.

playgroundTask.instructions: One of these emails is a phishing scam. Click the fake one.

items.label: From: mybank.com
items.preview: Your the cloud storage is almost full. You can upgrade your plan at icloud.com/upgrade. Monthly plans start at $0.99/month.

items.label: From: mybank-security-alert.net
items.preview: URGENT: Your account has been suspended! Click here immediately to verify your account: verify-account-now.net — or your account will be permanently deleted within 24 hours.

playgroundTask.fakeExplanation: Real bank emails come from @mybank.com — not 'mybank-security-alert.net'. Scams use urgency ('permanently deleted in 24 hours') and suspicious links to panic you into clicking.

drDigitalSuccess: You spotted the phishing email! Look at the sender's domain and be suspicious of urgent threats. Real companies don't threaten you like that.
drDigitalHint: Check the sender's email address carefully. Does it end in the real company's domain?
